specification.topic-statement: The internal tool registration process installs the four built-in agent-communication tools — fork, kill, send, and wait — into the shared tool registry using hardcoded JSON schemas.

scope.in-scope: The JSON schema definitions for each built-in tool, the sequence and method by which schemas are parsed and tools are added to the registry, and the association between each tool name and its handler and completion hook.
scope.boundary: The registry's storage and lookup behavior is managed by the tool registry subsystem. This topic only populates the registry; it does not define how tools are retrieved or dispatched.
The execution logic for each tool (fork handler, kill handler, send handler, wait handler) is implemented separately. This topic wires the associations but does not define the handler behaviors.
Registration is triggered by the shared context initialization and the registry refresh command. Those callers are out of scope here.

field.name: description
field.note: instructs the LLM that this creates a child agent with a specific task, inheriting parent history, working independently with its own LLM stream

field.name: parameters.name
field.note: required; short human-readable label for the child agent

field.name: parameters.prompt
field.note: required; the task for the child agent to work on

field.name: description
field.note: instructs the LLM that this terminates an agent and all its descendants, returning a list of killed UUIDs

field.name: parameters.uuid
field.note: required; UUID of the agent to terminate, described as cascading to all descendants

field.name: description
field.note: instructs the LLM that this sends a message to another agent retrievable with the wait tool

field.name: parameters.to
field.note: required; UUID of the recipient agent, described as 'must be running'

field.name: description
field.note: instructs the LLM that this waits for messages from other agents, supporting next-message and fan-in modes

field.name: parameters.timeout
field.note: required; maximum seconds to wait; 0 for non-blocking instant check

field.name: parameters.from_agents
field.note: optional; specific agent UUIDs for fan-in mode; omit to wait for next message from anyone

behavior.registration-sequence:
Registration parses all four schemas and registers all four tools in a fixed sequence: fork, kill, send, wait. The sequence is:
1. A temporary allocation context is created to hold schema string buffers during parsing. 2. For each of the four tools, the hardcoded JSON schema string is copied into the temporary context and parsed into an in-memory JSON document. The JSON allocator used for parsing is backed by the registry's own allocation context, so the parsed documents are owned by the registry. If parsing fails, the process panics immediately. 3. Each tool is registered with the registry under its name, with its parsed schema document, its handler, and its completion hook (if any). If registration fails, the process panics immediately. 4. The temporary allocation context is freed, releasing the schema string buffers. The parsed documents are unaffected because they were allocated under the registry context.
All failure paths during registration — schema parse failure, registry add failure — terminate the process via panic. There is no partial registration or graceful degradation.
behavior.completion-hooks:
Two of the four tools have completion hooks that run on the main thread after the tool handler returns from the background tool thread:
- The fork tool has a completion hook that transfers the newly created child agent from the tool thread's allocation context into the REPL's agent array. - The kill tool has a completion hook that sets the dead flag on the corresponding in-memory agent entry. - The send tool has no completion hook. - The wait tool has no completion hook.
The kill tool's schema description tells the LLM that termination "cascades to all descendants." The actual handler only terminates the directly named agent without cascading to any descendants. The "killed" array in the result always contains exactly one UUID.
behavior.schema-ownership: Schema JSON documents are allocated using a talloc-backed JSON allocator that attaches all allocations to the registry's context. This means the documents live for the lifetime of the registry and are freed when the registry is destroyed. The schema string buffers used during parsing are owned by the temporary context and are freed immediately after registration completes.
